_comment: Intake schema and TA placeholder catalog. The model authors variable content from the user's brief, assembles it into this JSON shape, then calls generate_proposal.py.

intake_schema.rfp_name: string — Opportunity / RFP name. Appears on cover and header.
intake_schema.customer_display_name: string — Customer name as it should appear throughout the proposal. Used in cover, body, headers, footers.
intake_schema.prepared_for: string — Contact name & title, or '[TO PROVIDE: contact name & title]' if unknown.
intake_schema.spoc_name: string — Sales SPOC name, or null (will become '[TO PROVIDE: Sales SPOC name]').
intake_schema.spoc_email: string — Sales SPOC email, or null (will become '[TO PROVIDE: Sales SPOC email]').

exec_summary.pleased: string — Paragraph 1: introduce iMocha's product as recommended platform.
exec_summary.aligned: string — Paragraph 2: restate customer requirements; surface explicit out-of-scope exclusions.
exec_summary.confident: string — Paragraph 3: proof points (hosting, integrations, certifications, commercial model).

intake_schema.scope_intro: string — One paragraph stating in-scope work + closing sentence restating exclusions.
deliverables: string — 8 to 12 bullet items mapping customer requirements to iMocha capabilities.
Include integrations the customer named, compliance, hosting/security, implementation, commercial model.
End with a final bullet restating explicit out-of-scope exclusions if any.

extra_substitutions._comment: Optional. Any extra token→value pairs the model wants to substitute. Useful if the master template has additional placeholders not in the standard catalog below.

ta_placeholder_catalog._note: These are the literal tokens present in the TA master (Skills-First_TA_Proposal_Template_v1_0.docx). Angle brackets are XML-escaped as < and > inside document.xml. The script handles substitution automatically; this catalog is for reference and debugging.

Customer Name (CUSTOMER NAME).where: Section 3 intro: 'outlined by Customer Name (CUSTOMER NAME)'
Customer Name (CUSTOMER NAME).ordering: MUST be substituted before the individual tokens it contains, otherwise the output reads 'NFCU (NFCU)'.

&lt;Customer Name&gt;.where: Cover page, body intros, multiple sections

CUSTOMER NAME.where: Section 3 sub-sections (assessment customization, branding, etc.) — 10 occurrences in TA master

&lt;How scope is aligned to what iMocha.where: Section 2 Scope of Work, intro paragraph

2.1 In scope Key Deliverables.where: Section 2.1 heading. In the master, this heading has no body content beneath it — section 3 follows directly.
2.1 In scope Key Deliverables.inject: 8-12 bullet paragraphs using the template's discovered bullet pattern (numId, font, size detected at runtime).

example_intake.rfp_name: Example Corp Skills Assessment Platform RFP

exec_summary.pleased: We are pleased to present iMocha's Skills-First Cloud for Talent Acquisition to Example Corp as the recommended platform for enterprise-grade candidate skills assessment across multiple job families, fully aligned to the requirements outlined in the RFP.
exec_summary.aligned: This proposal addresses Example Corp's core requirement — a secure, scalable assessment solution focused on cognitive ability, technical, and role-specific testing. Conversational AI Interview, Live Interview, and Asynchronous Pre-recorded Interview modules are not in core scope and may be evaluated as future add-ons. iMocha brings 3,000+ ready assessments and 35+ coding languages.
exec_summary.confident: iMocha is positioned to deliver measurable hiring outcomes through SaaS deployment on Microsoft Azure, native integration with Workday HCM, EEOC/ADA-aligned validity, WCAG 2.1 Level AA accessibility, ISO 27001 and SOC 2 Type II assurance, and a flexible annual subscription model with test-credit pricing.

example_intake.scope_intro: The scope of this engagement is the deployment, configuration, and rollout of iMocha's Skills Assessment platform for Example Corp. The engagement covers assessment library access, configurable design, candidate experience, anti-fraud controls, reporting and analytics, integration with Workday HCM, security and compliance posture, and onboarding, training, and ongoing support. Conversational AI Interview, Live Interview, and Asynchronous Pre-recorded Interview modules are explicitly out of scope for this engagement.
deliverables: Access to iMocha's assessment library covering cognitive, technical, and role-specific simulations — 3,000+ ready assessments and 35+ coding languages.
Configurable assessment creation, editing, packaging, and branding; assignment by role and requisition.
Anti-fraud and proctoring controls: ID verification, AI/video/image proctoring, Safe Assessment Browser, copy-paste restriction.
Reporting and analytics: dashboards, scoring, benchmarking, custom reporting.
Integration with Workday HCM via REST APIs.
Compliance and accessibility: EEOC/ADA, GDPR/CCPA, WCAG 2.1 Level AA.
Hosting and security: Microsoft Azure SaaS, TLS 1.2, 99.9% availability, RTO 1 hour, RPO 5 minutes, SSO, RBAC.
Implementation: onboarding, configuration, UAT, training, dedicated Customer Success Manager, 24x7 support.
Commercial: annual SaaS subscription with test-credit pricing and true-up.
Explicitly excluded: Conversational AI, Live, and Asynchronous Pre-recorded Interview modules.
